name: Coast Guard Air Station Sitka
tagline: The Coast Guard's air station in Southeast Alaska, flying rescue missions over the Inside Passage.
_note: Coast Guard Air Station Sitka (Baranof Island, SE Alaska). Region flagged OCONUS for browse UX, but Alaska is a BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger state -> KEEPS full template (BAH MHA AK401 Sitka; realestate + schools_live work). CG uses same BAH tables. Helicopter SAR over the Inside Passage/Gulf of Alaska. Remote island town (air/ferry only). Medical: CG clinic + local hospital + medevac. Schools: Sitka SD. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Alaska is TRICARE West. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 99835 (Sitka).

data.Role: Coast Guard air station for Southeast Alaska
data.Mission: Helicopter search and rescue over the Inside Passage and Gulf of Alaska
data.Access: Island town reached only by air or ferry

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Air Station Sitka flies helicopter search-and-rescue missions over Southeast Alaska's Inside Passage and the Gulf of Alaska, some of the most rugged maritime country in the nation.
It's in Sitka on Baranof Island, a small, beautiful, historic town reachable only by air or ferry, surrounded by mountains, ocean, and rainforest.

sections.title: A Remote Island Tour: Embrace Southeast Alaska

data.subtitle: Sitka is small, remote, and gorgeous, with a tiny housing market. Planning for island logistics and the wet climate matters more than picking a neighborhood.
intro: Sitka is a small island town with a very limited housing market and a wet maritime climate. Coast Guard housing helps a lot given the scarcity; some families also rent in town.
The big adjustments are the isolation (fly or ferry to leave), higher prices for shipped goods, and lots of rain, balanced against world-class outdoors right outside the door.

recommendations.advice: Given the tiny local market, Coast Guard housing is valuable here; coordinate early.
Sitka itself is the only option; the rental market is small, so start the search early.
No roads out: budget for air/ferry travel and higher prices on shipped goods.
Fishing, hiking, and wildlife are extraordinary; an outdoors mindset makes the tour.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing is important here given Sitka's very limited rental market; some families also rent in town.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office as early as possible for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA AK401 (Sitka) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. The Sitka School District serves the community.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, Air Station Sitka
data.Care: On-base clinic care plus the local Sitka hospital; complex care requires medevac to Anchorage, Seattle, or beyond

data.Commissary: Limited; most groceries via local Sitka stores (no large DoD commissary)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. In tiny, remote Sitka most families use Coast Guard housing or rent, given the very limited market.
rent: Your tour is short or your orders feel uncertain.
You prefer CG housing or renting in a very small market.
You'd rather not own where inventory and resale are extremely limited.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in town.
buy: You expect a longer tour and want to settle on the island.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity.
You're comfortable with island logistics, weather, and a tiny resale market.
You've found a rare home you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Sitka's tiny, isolated market leads most families to CG housing or renting. Buying is uncommon and carries real resale risk. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics, and a remote island move adds shipping and travel planning. Start early.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging before you arrive; options are very limited. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you settle in.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Lock in housing
steps.text: CG housing is key here given the tiny market; coordinate as early as possible.

steps.title: Plan your vehicle and travel
steps.text: Arrange vehicle shipping/ferry early (no roads into Sitka), and prep for a wet climate. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the Sitka School District early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment; complex care means medevac off the island, so understand the referral process.

data.a: It's in Alaska, a U.S. state, so it uses stateside pay tools like BAH (not OHA). We group it under the OCONUS/Alaska section for browsing, but it's remote: a fly-or-ferry-only island town.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Sitka is MHA AK401.
Mostly in Coast Guard housing or renting in Sitka, since the local market is tiny.
Remote and rainy but stunning: mountains, ocean, world-class fishing and wildlife, reached only by air or ferry.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. The island is remote, so tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.
